title: Phanera piperifolia
taxonomy: Phanera piperifolia is a species of plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to tropical forests in Southeast Asia and is also known as the "piper-leaf tree". The classification of Phanera piperifolia is as follows: it belongs to the genus Phanera, which is a genus of evergreen trees and shrubs. The genus Phanera is further classified under the subfamily Rauvoloideae. More research is needed for a comprehensive understanding of its classification.

intro: Phanera piperifolia, also known as the Indian licorice vine, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Anacardiaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Phanera piperifolia include alkaloids, glycosides, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Phanera piperifolia is often made into decoctions, infusions, or extracts, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, diarrhea, and eczema.
